name: Primordial black hole dark matter
summary: Black holes formed in the early universe (typically during the radiation-dominated era from collapse of large-amplitude curvature perturbations) can constitute some or all of the dark matter. Allowed mass windows after combined microlensing (EROS, OGLE, Subaru HSC), CMB-distortion (Planck mu-distortion, FIRAS), dynamical (wide binaries, ultra-faint dwarfs), and gravitational-wave (LIGO/Virgo merger rate) constraints: asteroid-mass window 10^-16 - 10^-11 M_sun is the only region where PBHs can be 100% of DM; sub-percent contributions allowed in stellar-mass range relevant to LIGO/Virgo binary BH mergers. Production mechanisms include inflaton-spectrum-bump models, axion-curvaton mechanisms, and first-order phase transition bubble collisions.

addresses_puzzles.note: PBH in asteroid-mass window (10^17-10^22 g) consistent with 100% DM after Subaru HSC microlensing (Niikura et al. 2019 arXiv:1701.02151) and femtolensing (Katz et al. 2018). Stellar-mass PBHs (1-100 M_sun) constrained to f_PBH < 0.01 (CMB accretion + LIGO merger rate) - cannot be all DM but can contribute to LIGO mergers.

Hawking-evaporating light PBHs (M < 10^11 g) inject baryon-number asymmetric particles preferentially if CP-violating physics is in the spectrum (Toussaint-Treiman-Wilczek-Zee 1979); revived as 'PBH baryogenesis' (Hook 2014, arXiv:1404.0113; Hamada-Iso 2016).

touches_benchmarks.note: PBH DM does not produce direct-detection signal (no particle-nuclear cross-section). Provides a 'null channel' DM candidate consistent with all WIMP-direct-detection null results.

new_scales: PBH formation epoch z_form (set by perturbation horizon-entry)
introduces.note: PBH mass M_PBH (or mass function) and fraction f_PBH = rho_PBH / rho_DM. Inflation-spectrum-bump models add inflaton-potential parameters but those are absorbed into existing inflation-mechanism counts.

history: Zeldovich-Novikov (1967) first noted PBH formation. Hawking (1971) MNRAS 152, 75. Carr-Hawking (1974), MNRAS 168, 399. Modern revival post-LIGO GW150914: Bird, Cholis, Munoz, Ali-Haimoud, Kamionkowski, Kovetz, Raccanelli, Riess (2016), 'Did LIGO detect dark matter?', PRL 116, 201301, arXiv:1603.00464. Comprehensive constraint review: Carr, Kuhnel, Sandstad (2016), PRD 94, 083504, arXiv:1607.06077; Green-Kavanagh (2021) review arXiv:2007.10722.

references.citation: Carr and Hawking, 'Black holes in the early universe', MNRAS 168, 399 (1974)
Bird et al., 'Did LIGO detect dark matter?', Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 201301 (2016)
Carr, Kuhnel, Sandstad, 'Primordial black holes as dark matter', Phys. Rev. D 94, 083504 (2016)
